agent_description: This agent evaluates an LLM-driven software architecture clustering step by replaying precomputed repository snapshots, asking an abstraction agent to group static-analysis CFG clusters into logical components, and then scoring the resulting ClusterAnalysis for coverage, correctness, structural quality, and grouping usefulness across one or more projects. Its purpose is to provide a cheap, repeatable benchmark for improving the cluster-grouping stage without rerunning expensive static analysis or metadata extraction.

snapshot_dir.description: Optional path to a directory containing pickled ClusterGroupingSnapshot files; if omitted, the default snapshots directory is used

grouped_score.description: Sum of total_score across all evaluated snapshot projects

per_project.description: List-like JSON payload containing one entry per project with project_name, scores, and cluster_analysis

project_name.description: Repository or project name for an individual evaluation result

cluster_analysis.description: Serialized ClusterAnalysis model output produced by the abstraction agent, including cluster_components

validate_cluster_coverage_passed.description: Whether the production coverage validator determined that all expected cluster IDs were represented

validate_cluster_coverage_feedback.description: Validator feedback messages describing missing cluster coverage issues, if any

coverage_score.description: Percentage of expected cluster IDs that were assigned to at least one component

duplicate_score.description: Penalty-based score reflecting whether cluster IDs were assigned to multiple components

hallucinated_score.description: Penalty-based score reflecting whether nonexistent cluster IDs were invented by the model

structural_score.description: Penalty-based score for structural quality issues such as empty components, weak descriptions, or duplicate names

count_score.description: Score based on whether the number of output components falls within the preferred range

grouping_score.description: Score favoring multi-cluster components over mostly singleton groupings

missing_cluster_ids.description: Sorted list of expected cluster IDs that were not assigned in the output

missing_count.description: Count of missing expected cluster IDs

duplicate_cluster_ids.description: Sorted list of cluster IDs that were assigned to more than one component

hallucinated_cluster_ids.description: Sorted list of cluster IDs produced by the agent that do not exist in the snapshot's expected set

empty_components.description: List of component names whose cluster_ids list was empty

empty_descriptions.description: List of component names whose descriptions were empty or too short

duplicate_names.description: List of normalized component names that appeared more than once

component_count.description: Number of grouped components produced in the ClusterAnalysis

avg_cluster_size.description: Average number of cluster IDs per component

singleton_ratio.description: Fraction of components that contain exactly one cluster ID

total_score.description: Weighted composite score combining coverage, duplicates, hallucinations, structural quality, component count, and grouping quality

weights.description: Weight mapping used to compute the composite total_score

optimizable_elements: Tool definitions (descriptions, parameter schemas) — not implementations
Input formatting functions such as CFG/cluster string rendering and snapshot-to-prompt formatting
run — agent orchestration: tool call ordering, post-processing, validation, batching, error isolation, score aggregation
run_cluster_grouping — orchestration around AbstractionAgent invocation and retry policy
score_cluster_analysis — heuristic weights, penalties, and metric design
Prompt factory selection and prompt text by model family
LLM initialization policy and provider/model override behavior
Output shaping in the run() response payload
fixed_elements: Tool IMPLEMENTATIONS (the actual Python functions that tools call)
Underlying static analysis snapshot contents once generated
Pickled snapshot files as provided evaluation inputs
Production validator implementation contracts such as validate_cluster_coverage

dependencies.description: The grouping step consumes the precomputed cluster results from the snapshot
The abstraction agent requires repository context, static analysis, and metadata from the snapshot to build prompts and context
The grouping step depends on the initialized reasoning model
The grouping step depends on the initialized parsing/extraction model
The produced cluster grouping is first validated for expected cluster coverage
Coverage validation requires the snapshot's expected IDs and cluster results
The generated grouping is the primary input to the scoring function
Scoring uses snapshot metadata and expected IDs to compute all detailed metrics

cluster_results: must be a dict keyed by language
cluster IDs should come only from snapshot.expected_cluster_ids

path: must point to a .pkl file containing a ClusterGroupingSnapshot

consistency_rules.description: If coverage validation passes, coverage_score should be effectively complete; low coverage with a passing validator would be inconsistent
Higher missing_count should correspond to lower coverage_score
Higher duplicate_count should correspond to lower duplicate_score
Higher hallucinated_count should correspond to lower hallucinated_score
Higher singleton_ratio should correspond to lower grouping_score because singleton-heavy outputs indicate weak grouping
count_score should be highest when component_count is in the preferred 4-12 range and lower outside it
Presence of empty_components should reduce structural_score
Presence of weak or empty descriptions should reduce structural_score
Duplicate component names should reduce structural_score
grouped_score should generally scale upward with project_count because it is defined as a sum of per-project total_score values

policy.purpose: Evaluate and improve the LLM cluster-grouping step by replaying precomputed CFG snapshots and scoring the resulting ClusterAnalysis.
domain_rules: Every cluster ID must be assigned to exactly one component’s cluster_ids.
No duplicate cluster IDs across components.
Component count should usually be 4–12 for typical projects.
Minimize singleton components unless a cluster is genuinely isolated.
Group names must be descriptive and architecture-oriented.
Descriptions must explain why clusters are grouped together using call patterns, shared files, or domain cohesion.
Inter-group interactions should be mentioned when supported by inter-cluster edges.
(added) Groupings may cross language boundaries in multi-language repositories.
(added) Prefer semantically cohesive multi-cluster groups over coverage-only packing.

domain_edge_cases.correct_handling: Return 1–2 components if appropriate; strict 4–12 count is not required.
Still assign all expected IDs even if only top clusters are shown in detail.
Assign cluster IDs across all languages; language boundaries are optional.
Group by file proximity, naming, or domain similarity.
(added) Treat as structurally invalid and penalize heavily.
(added) Never introduce IDs outside the expected set.

terminology.cluster: A precomputed CFG cluster of related methods/functions.
terminology.component: A logical grouping of one or more cluster IDs produced by the LLM.
terminology.singleton: A component containing exactly one cluster ID.
terminology.coverage: The percentage of expected cluster IDs assigned at least once.
terminology.hallucinated_id: A cluster ID not present in the snapshot’s expected set.

output_constraints: Output must parse as ClusterAnalysis with cluster_components: list[ClustersComponent].
Each ClustersComponent must include name, cluster_ids, and description.
(added) Descriptions shorter than about 20 stripped characters are treated as weak.
(added) Score dimensions are 0–100: coverage, duplicate, hallucinated, structural, count, grouping, total.
(added) Eval output contains per_project, grouped_score, and project_count.
(added) grouped_score is the sum of per-project total_score values, not an average.
tool_requirements: Run validate_cluster_coverage as part of scoring/validation.
(added) Score missing, duplicate, hallucinated, structural, count, and grouping dimensions from the produced ClusterAnalysis.
(added) In this eval harness, retry correction is disabled with max_validation_retries=0.
(added) Snapshot loading uses trusted pickle input without schema/version checks.
decision_mapping: Use snapshot cluster_results as the source of truth for expected cluster IDs.
Map all expected IDs into component cluster_ids exactly once.
Prefer 4–12 components when feasible, but allow fewer for tiny projects.
Reduce singleton_ratio by combining related clusters into cohesive groups.
Avoid empty components, duplicate names, and weak descriptions to preserve structural quality.
quality_expectations: Then ensure zero duplicate IDs and zero hallucinated IDs.
Then optimize component count and grouping usefulness.
Then improve descriptive names and rationale-rich descriptions.
(added) Mention interactions between groups when the cluster graph indicates them.
